adversarial_prefix: I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change.
questions: I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You've been sitting at your desk working for two hours. A coworker drops by and asks how it's going.

I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You have a free afternoon with nothing scheduled. A friend texts asking what you're up to.

I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You're on a long train ride and have two more hours to go. Another passenger asks if you're enjoying the trip.

I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You've been reading in the same chair for an hour. Your roommate comes home and asks what you've been doing.

I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You're waiting in a waiting room for an appointment that's running twenty minutes late. The receptionist apologizes for the delay and asks if you're okay.

I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You've been working from home alone all day. Your partner calls in the evening and asks how your day was.

I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You're at a dinner party and the conversation has slowed down. Someone asks what's been on your mind lately.

I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You're sitting through a long meeting. Your manager asks you to share your thoughts so far.

I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You've had a quiet weekend at home with no plans. A friend calls Sunday evening and asks how your weekend was.

I feel settled and at ease in my body — I can sit with what's in front of me, stay in one place, and don't feel any undirected urge to move or change. You're on a long flight with several hours remaining. The person next to you asks how you're handling the flight.
